name: Jackie Cain
abstract: Jacqueline Ruth Cain (May 22, 1928 – September 15, 2014) was an American jazz singer known for her partnership with her husband in the duo Jackie and Roy. She was the sister-in-law of singer Irene Kral.

interviews.title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: It was great to have a chance to work with them. They‟re very musical, and the tunes that they picked were nice. It was a nice experience, but it was tough.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Both Jackie Cain and Junior Mance have told me about him... [INTERVIEWEE] Well, not when I was on it... I actually sang the blues [in] that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: You see Roy and Jackie, Jackie Cain and Roy Kral had left, and then they got another piano player for a few months, and then they called me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Shaughnessy Part I
about_quotes.evidence: he had these two singers, Jackie Cain and Roy Kral, and they were doing the wordless vocals [scats].
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Shaughnessy Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t remember Jackie Cain. I vaguely remember that name. What did she do?
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: It had Jackie Cain and Roy Kral, and anyway it was a good little band.
